mechanism_narrative: A falling 3-month trend in FEDFUNDS lowers the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows and raises risk appetite, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when the rate trend is declining.
evidence_refs: fact:Decision snapshot reports FEDFUNDS at 0.09 as of the latest available month
failure_condition: Reject if falling 3-month FEDFUNDS changes do not produce higher 20-day S&P 500 returns than rising FEDFUNDS changes in walk-forward tests, or if Rank IC is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in UNRATE signals an adverse labor-income shock that reduces household spending and expected corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days after the unemployment deterioration is observed.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:Decision snapshot reports UNRATE at 5.8 as of the latest available month
failure_condition: Reject if triggered UNRATE increases are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of walk-forward instances or if triggered average returns are not below non-triggered returns at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in RSAFS while FEDFUNDS remains near the zero lower bound signals improving consumer demand supported by easy policy, leading analysts to revise earnings upward and producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 1-month growth is positive and FEDFUNDS is below 0.25
evidence_refs: fact:Decision snapshot reports RSAFS at 449282 and FEDFUNDS at 0.09
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with accommodative monetary policy
failure_condition: Reject if the joint RSAFS-positive and FEDFUNDS-below-0.25 condition does not outperform periods with weak RSAFS by at least 1 percentage point in average 20-day returns over rolling 3-year walk-forward windows.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO crosses above its 3-month moving average after a contraction, factory utilization and operating leverage are improving, which raises expected margins and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO crosses above its 3-month moving average after being below it for at least 2 months
evidence_refs: fact:Decision snapshot reports INDPRO at 106.6617 as of the latest available month
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO recovery crosses are followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of triggered cases or if the triggered mean return is not higher than the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: A large 3-month decline in HOUST signals weakening housing demand, tighter credit transmission, and falling durable-goods activity, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls by more than 10 percent over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:Decision snapshot reports HOUST at 1009.0 as of the latest available month
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST declines greater than 10 percent over 3 months are followed by non-negative 60-day S&P 500 returns on average, or if hit-rate for negative returns is below 50% in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Low CPIAUCSL inflation combined with FEDFUNDS below 0.25 indicates the central bank can maintain emergency accommodation without an inflation constraint, supporting equity multiples and producing positive 20-day S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation is below 1 percent and FEDFUNDS is below 0.25
evidence_refs: fact:Decision snapshot reports CPIAUCSL at 237.642 and FEDFUNDS at 0.09
failure_condition: Reject if this low-inflation and low-FEDFUNDS interaction has lower average 20-day S&P 500 returns than periods with FEDFUNDS below 0.25 alone, or if interaction coefficient is not positive in walk-forward regressions.

mechanism_narrative: A PAYEMS month-over-month gain after prior payroll losses signals that firms have resumed hiring, improving income expectations and short-term risk sentiment, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 1-month change crosses from negative to positive after at least 2 negative monthly changes
evidence_refs: fact:Decision snapshot reports PAYEMS at 140045.0 as of the latest available month
failure_condition: Reject if PAYEMS positive-cross triggers are not followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in at least 55% of walk-forward cases or if the triggered mean return is below the unconditional 5-day mean.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in GDPC1 combined with elevated UNRATE identifies a recessionary demand regime in which earnings downgrades dominate policy support, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 1-quarter growth is negative and UNRATE is above its 12-month moving average
evidence_refs: fact:Decision snapshot reports GDPC1 at 16164.1 and UNRATE at 5.8
failure_condition: Reject if the GDPC1-negative and UNRATE-elevated regime produces positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of triggered cases or if its mean return is not below expansion-regime returns.
